name: Fix Agent Daemon Status Synchronization
description: Fix the issue where the Settings page shows 'Agent Daemon: Stopped' even when the daemon is running (started via start.sh). The daemon status check only tracks daemons started via the API endpoint, not those started manually.

tasks.name: Modify client daemon to write PID file on startup
tasks.description: Update /root/projects/agents-manager/client/main.py to write a PID file when the daemon starts in daemon mode. The PID file should be written to a predictable location (e.g., /tmp/agents-manager-daemon.pid or in the client directory). Also ensure the PID file is removed on graceful shutdown.

key_changes: Import os and signal modules for PID file management
Add write_pid_file() function to write PID to file
Add remove_pid_file() function to clean up on shutdown
Call write_pid_file() at the start of run_daemon() function
Call remove_pid_file() in the finally block of run_daemon() function
Add signal handler to ensure PID file cleanup on SIGTERM/SIGINT

steps: Start the daemon manually: bash start.sh
Check that PID file exists: cat /tmp/agents-manager-daemon.pid
Verify PID matches actual daemon process: ps aux | grep 'python main.py --daemon'
Stop the daemon and verify PID file is removed

tasks.name: Update API daemon status check to use PID file
tasks.description: Modify /root/projects/agents-manager/api/src/routes/daemon.ts to check the PID file when the internal daemonProcess reference is null. Update getDaemonStatus() function to: 1) First check if daemonProcess is set (current behavior), 2) If not, read the PID file, 3) If PID file exists, verify the process is running using process.kill(pid, 0), 4) Return 'running' if process exists, 'stopped' otherwise.

key_changes: Import fs module for reading PID file
Update getDaemonStatus() function to check PID file as fallback
Update GET /api/daemon/status to return PID from file if available

implementation_details.checkProcessRunning: Use process.kill(pid, 0) wrapped in try-catch. If no error, process is running. If error (ESRCH), process not found.
implementation_details.readPidFile: Use fs.readFileSync(PID_FILE, 'utf-8') and parse as integer. Handle errors if file doesn't exist or is invalid.
implementation_details.return_pid: When daemon is running via PID file, still return the PID in the response so UI can display it.

steps: Ensure daemon is NOT running (kill if needed)
Start daemon via start.sh
Call API: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer dev-token-change-in-production' http://localhost:3000/api/daemon/status
Verify response shows status: 'running' and includes PID
Stop daemon and verify status returns to 'stopped'

tasks.name: Update POST /api/daemon/start to write PID file
tasks.description: When the daemon is started via the API endpoint, also write the PID file so the status detection works consistently. Update the POST /api/daemon/start handler to write the PID file after spawning the process.

key_changes: Import fs module if not already imported
After spawning daemonProcess, write its PID to the PID file
Handle cleanup of PID file in daemonProcess.on('exit') handler

steps: Ensure daemon is NOT running
Start daemon via API: curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: Bearer dev-token-change-in-production' http://localhost:3000/api/daemon/start
Verify PID file exists: cat /tmp/agents-manager-daemon.pid
Verify status shows running via API call

tasks.name: Update POST /api/daemon/stop to clean up PID file
tasks.description: When stopping the daemon via the API, also remove the PID file. Update the POST /api/daemon/stop handler to delete the PID file after killing the process.

key_changes: After daemonProcess.kill('SIGTERM'), also delete the PID file
Use fs.unlinkSync(PID_FILE) wrapped in try-catch to handle case where file doesn't exist
Also clean up PID file in the daemonProcess.on('exit') handler as backup

steps: Start daemon (either via start.sh or API)
Stop daemon via API: curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: Bearer dev-token-change-in-production' http://localhost:3000/api/daemon/stop
Verify PID file is removed: ls /tmp/agents-manager-daemon.pid (should fail)
Verify status shows stopped

tasks.name: Test end-to-end daemon status synchronization
tasks.description: Comprehensive testing of the daemon status synchronization fix. Test all scenarios: starting via start.sh, starting via API, stopping via API, stopping via kill, and verifying the Settings page displays correct status in all cases.

test_scenarios.name: Manual start via start.sh
steps: Ensure daemon is stopped
Run: bash start.sh
Open Settings page in browser (http://localhost:5173/settings)
Verify 'Agent Daemon: Running (PID XXXXX)' is displayed
Verify green pulsing status indicator

test_scenarios.name: API start
steps: Ensure daemon is stopped
Start via API: curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: Bearer dev-token-change-in-production' http://localhost:3000/api/daemon/start
Open Settings page
Verify status shows Running with PID

test_scenarios.name: API stop
steps: Ensure daemon is running
Stop via API: curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: Bearer dev-token-change-in-production' http://localhost:3000/api/daemon/stop
Open Settings page
Verify status shows Stopped with gray indicator

test_scenarios.name: Manual stop (Ctrl+C)
steps: Start daemon via start.sh
Press Ctrl+C to stop
Refresh Settings page
Verify status shows Stopped (after 5 second polling interval)

test_scenarios.name: Process killed externally
steps: Start daemon via start.sh
Kill process manually: kill $(cat /tmp/agents-manager-daemon.pid)
Refresh Settings page
Verify status shows Stopped within 5-10 seconds
Run all test scenarios above
Document any issues found
Verify Settings page always shows correct daemon status
Confirm PID file is properly created and cleaned up
